payload.title: Spike: multi-tenant auth & identity model
payload.description:
OPEN QUESTION: What replaces the LAN trust model for a multi-tenant public service?

The current model — loopback bypass + per-hub bearer token + LAN-scoped self-signed CA with mTLS device pairing — assumes a single trust domain and CANNOT be stretched to multi-tenant. This is the largest genuinely-new surface and touches every endpoint, so design it early.

Questions to resolve:
- Identity provider: OAuth/OIDC (Google/GitHub/Apple) vs email+password vs both? What do our users expect?
- Session model: JWT vs server sessions? Token lifetime/refresh? How do CLI and iOS authenticate non-interactively (API keys / device tokens)?
- How does the EXISTING mTLS device-pairing flow ([[51]]) relate to cloud auth — keep it for LAN, layer cloud identity on top, or unify?
- Account <-> workspace <-> team mapping. Who owns a workspace? How is it shared? Permission model (owner/member/viewer)?
- How is per-tenant isolation enforced at the data layer so one tenant can never read another's events?
- Authorization on event upload: how do we verify the 'actor' field on incoming events matches the authenticated principal?

Deliverable: comment with a proposed auth architecture (or 2-3 options w/ tradeoffs). Likely spawns child stories: 'Auth provider integration', 'CLI/iOS non-interactive auth', 'Tenant isolation at data layer', and possibly its own ADR under docs/adr/.
